---
title: "Vector Exercise"
# Purpose: Provide beginner practice with vectors, summaries, and logical filtering.
---

{{< include ../_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}

<!-- Purpose: This page gives short editable exercises that run in the browser. -->

Use the code cells below to practice vector basics. Edit the code, run it, and
compare the output with what you expected.

## Exercise 1

Create a vector of leaf counts, then calculate the mean and maximum.

```{webr}
leaf_count <- c(4, 5, 5, 7, 8, 9)

# Add code below this line.
mean(leaf_count)
max(leaf_count)
```

## Exercise 2

Select only the plants with at least 7 leaves.

```{webr}
leaf_count <- c(4, 5, 5, 7, 8, 9)

# Change the threshold and run the cell again.
threshold <- 7
leaf_count[leaf_count >= threshold]
```

## Exercise 3

Use vector indexing to compare the first and last measurements.

```{webr}
height_cm <- c(12.1, 14.5, 13.8, 16.2, 15.4)

first_height <- height_cm[1]
last_height <- height_cm[length(height_cm)]

last_height - first_height
```

## Optional challenge

Use the bundled CSV and calculate the mean height for day 21 only.

```{webr}
plants <- read.csv("data/plant_growth.csv")

day_21 <- plants[plants$day == 21, ]
mean(day_21$height_cm)
```
